toast.araxers_bestiary.observation.title: Bestiary: New Observation!
screen.araxers_bestiary.unlock_tabs.undi: Defeat, observe with a Spyglass, tame, trade, feed or breed to unlock details.
screen.araxers_bestiary.unlock_tabs.sigh: Sighted mobs have increased chance to gain higher ranks
screen.araxers_bestiary.tooltip.sighted: Sighted mobs have increased chance to gain higher ranks.
entity.araxers_bestiary.bestiary.minecra: The Allay is a small, friendly, blue flying mob that collects items matching what the player gives it. It delivers these items to note blocks or the player who gave it the item. Allays can be found in pillager outposts, woodland mansions, and ancient cities. They love music and will dance when near a jukebox playing a record.

Axolotls are passive aquatic mobs found in lush caves. They come in five different color variants: pink, brown, gold, cyan, and the rare blue. Axolotls attack most underwater hostile mobs and can be picked up in a water bucket. They play dead when injured and receive regeneration after fighting. Axolotls were the first amphibian mob added to Minecraft.

Bats are small, passive ambient mobs that spawn in dark areas underground. They serve a purely atmospheric purpose and don't drop anything when defeated. Bats hang upside down when idle and fly around erratically when disturbed or during nighttime. They're the only naturally-occurring flying passive mob in the game. Bats will appear more frequently during the Halloween season.

Bees are neutral flying mobs that live in bee nests and beehives. They collect pollen from flowers and help grow crops through pollination. Bees produce honey, which can be collected with bottles, and honeycomb, which can be harvested with shears. They become hostile when attacked or when their nest/hive is disturbed, and they die after stinging once. Bees cannot fly in rain and return to their home at night.

Blazes are hostile mobs native to the Nether. They appear as floating, fiery rods surrounded by smaller rods that rotate around their body. Blazes attack by shooting fireballs at players, which can set them on fire. They spawn naturally in nether fortresses and are immune to fire and lava damage. Blaze rods, which they drop when defeated, are essential for brewing and accessing the End dimension.

Camels are passive mobs that spawn in desert villages. They can be tamed and ridden like horses, but with unique abilities. Camels can carry two players at once, can dash forward in a sprint, and have a high enough stance that hostile mobs cannot reach riders. They have a distinctive sitting animation and can be bred using cactus. Camels are immune to cacti damage and can walk over fences without jumping.

Cats are passive tamable mobs that spawn in villages and swamp huts. They come in 11 different coat patterns including tabby, tuxedo, red, and siamese. Cats can be tamed using raw cod or salmon and will follow players who hold these items. Tamed cats will sit on beds and chests, scare away phantoms, and occasionally bring gifts to their owners in the morning. They also scare away creepers, making them useful for base defense.
(gifted by a cat)

Cave spiders are smaller, more dangerous variants of regular spiders. They spawn from spawners in abandoned mineshafts. Unlike regular spiders, cave spiders can fit through 1-block gaps and inflict poison on players when they attack. They're hostile in light levels of 11 or less, and neutral in brighter light. Cave spiders can climb walls and are 3/4 the size of regular spiders.

Chickens are passive farm animals that lay eggs and can be bred with seeds. They spawn naturally in the Overworld in grassy biomes. Chickens drop feathers and raw chicken when defeated, and they lay eggs approximately every 5-10 minutes. They take fall damage very slowly due to their ability to flap their wings. Baby chickens follow adult chickens, and chickens can be led using seeds like wheat, beetroot, or melon seeds.

Cod are passive aquatic mobs that spawn in cold, lukewarm, and deep ocean biomes. They swim in schools and flee from players and predators like dolphins and guardians. Cod can be caught with a water bucket to create a bucket of cod, which can be placed to release the cod. When defeated, they drop raw cod and occasionally bone meal. They're one of the most common fish in the game and are an important food source.

Cows are passive farm animals that spawn in grassy biomes in the Overworld. They can be bred using wheat and provide leather, beef, and milk. Players can use an empty bucket on a cow to collect milk, which can be used to make cake or to cure certain status effects. Cows follow players who hold wheat, and baby cows take 20 minutes to grow into adults.

Creepers are iconic hostile mobs known for their silent approach and explosive nature. They silently stalk players and explode when close, destroying blocks and causing damage. Creepers fear cats and ocelots, which will cause them to flee. When struck by lightning, they become charged creepers with a more powerful explosion. If defeated by a skeleton's arrow, they drop a music disc. Creepers are one of the most dangerous and recognizable mobs in Minecraft.
(If defeated by a skeleton's arrow)

Dolphins are neutral aquatic mobs that spawn in ocean biomes. They swim in groups and can lead players to underwater structures like shipwrecks and ocean ruins when fed raw cod or salmon. Dolphins grant players the "Dolphin's Grace" effect when swimming near them, which increases swimming speed. They jump out of water, play with items, and attack hostile mobs like drowned. Dolphins need to surface for air periodically or they will take damage.

Donkeys are passive tamable mobs that spawn in plains and savanna biomes. They can be tamed by repeatedly mounting them until hearts appear. Unlike horses, donkeys can be equipped with chests to carry items, making them useful for transportation and storage. They can be bred with horses to produce mules, which combine traits of both parents. Donkeys can be equipped with saddles for riding and can be led with a lead.

Drowned are hostile underwater zombie variants that spawn in oceans, rivers, and underwater ruins. They can also be created when zombies are submerged in water for 30 seconds. Drowned can swim and survive underwater indefinitely. Some drowned spawn with tridents, which they can throw at players from a distance. They burn in sunlight unless they're in water or wearing a helmet. Drowned are one of the few sources of tridents in the game and also have a chance to drop copper ingots and gold ingots.
(If equipped with a trident)

Elder guardians are powerful hostile aquatic mobs that spawn in ocean monuments. They are larger variants of regular guardians and are much more dangerous. Only three elder guardians spawn in each ocean monument - one in the central chamber and two in the wings. They attack with a laser beam and can inflict the Mining Fatigue III effect on players who come near the monument, making it difficult to break blocks. Elder guardians drop prismarine shards, prismarine crystals, and wet sponges when defeated, making them the only renewable source of sponges in the game.

The Ender Dragon is the main boss of Minecraft, residing in the End dimension. This large, black dragon flies around the central island of the End, guarded by End Crystals that heal it. Players must destroy these crystals before effectively damaging the dragon. It attacks by charging at players, shooting dragon fireballs, and using a breath attack that leaves lingering Dragon's Breath, which can be collected in glass bottles. When defeated for the first time, it drops a Dragon Egg and creates an exit portal. The dragon can be respawned using End Crystals placed on the exit portal.
(Only dropped once after defeating the Ender Dragon for the first time)
(Collected with a glass bottle during the fight)

Endermen are tall, neutral mobs that can teleport and pick up certain blocks. They spawn in all three dimensions: the Overworld, the Nether, and the End, with the End being their home dimension. Endermen become hostile when a player looks directly at their upper body or attacks them. They take damage from water and projectiles, but can teleport away from danger. Endermen drop ender pearls when defeated, which are essential for accessing the End dimension. They're one of the most iconic and mysterious mobs in Minecraft.

Endermites are tiny hostile mobs that have a small chance to spawn when a player uses an Ender Pearl. These purple, bug-like creatures are related to Endermen and will attack players on sight. They're very small, making them difficult to hit, and can fit through 1-block gaps. Endermites don't drop any items when defeated. Endermen will attack Endermites if they see them, suggesting some kind of natural enmity between the species.

Evokers are powerful illager spellcasters that spawn in woodland mansions and during village raids. They attack using magic, summoning fangs from the ground that snap at players and vexes (small flying hostile spirits) to fight for them. Evokers can also turn nearby sheep blue or red as a defensive measure. They always drop a Totem of Undying when defeated, which is a valuable item that can save a player from death once when held. Evokers are considered one of the most dangerous illager types due to their magical abilities.

Foxes are skittish passive mobs that spawn in taiga biomes. They come in two variants: red (in normal taigas) and white/arctic (in snowy taigas). Foxes are nocturnal, sleeping during the day and becoming active at night. They're naturally afraid of players but can be bred using sweet berries, and the offspring will trust the player who bred them. Foxes can pick up items in their mouths, attack chickens and rabbits, and have a unique pouncing attack to catch prey. They're one of the few mobs that can jump over fences and can sometimes be found sleeping in snow during snowfall.

Frogs are passive amphibian mobs that spawn in swamp and mangrove swamp biomes. They come in three variants based on the biome they grow up in: temperate (orange), cold (green), and warm (white). Frogs can jump, swim, and walk on lily pads. They eat small slimes and magma cubes by using their tongue to catch them. When a frog eats a small slime, it produces a slime ball, and when it eats a magma cube, it produces a froglight block (a colored light source that varies by frog variant). Frogs can be bred using slimeballs, and they lay frogspawn that hatches into tadpoles, which grow into frogs.
(If eaten a small slime)
(If eaten a small magma cube)

Ghasts are large, floating hostile mobs native to the Nether. They appear as giant, pale cubes with nine tentacles hanging beneath them and are known for their distinctive crying and wailing sounds. Ghasts attack by shooting explosive fireballs at players from a distance, which can be deflected back at them with well-timed hits. They can fly and pass through blocks, making them difficult to avoid in open areas of the Nether. When defeated, Ghasts always drop gunpowder and have a chance to drop ghast tears, which are used in brewing potions of regeneration and are essential for creating end crystals.

Giants are unused hostile mobs that appear as oversized zombies, standing six times taller than a regular zombie. They don't spawn naturally in the game and can only be summoned using commands. Despite their intimidating appearance, Giants don't have any AI in the current version of Minecraft, so they don't move or attack players. They're essentially a remnant from earlier versions of the game that was never fully implemented. Giants were originally intended to be a challenging enemy but were left incomplete. They have no drops and no natural spawning locations.

Glow Squids are passive aquatic mobs that were added to Minecraft in the Caves & Cliffs update after winning a community mob vote. They are a variant of the regular squid but with a bioluminescent appearance that makes them glow in dark underwater environments. Despite their name and appearance, they don't actually emit light that illuminates the surrounding area. Glow Squids spawn in underwater caves, underground lakes, and the deep dark biome. When defeated, they drop glow ink sacs, which can be used to make signs and item frames glow in the dark, creating unique decoration options.

Goats are neutral mobs that spawn in mountainous biomes. They can jump very high (up to 10 blocks) and have a unique ramming attack where they charge at players or mobs if provoked. There are two variants: regular goats and screaming goats, which are more aggressive and make a distinctive screaming sound. When a screaming goat rams into certain blocks, it has a chance to drop a goat horn, which players can use to make trumpet sounds. Goats can be bred using wheat and can be milked like cows. They're known for their unpredictable behavior and tendency to headbutt anything that stays near them for too long.
(When a screaming goat rams into a block)

Guardians are hostile aquatic mobs that spawn in ocean monuments. They have a spiky, fish-like appearance with a single large eye and can swim in any direction. Guardians attack by shooting a laser beam at players, which has a charging period before firing and can penetrate blocks. They can also extend their spikes to deal contact damage to players who touch them. Guardians are one of the main defenders of ocean monuments, along with elder guardians. They're immune to drowning and can see players even through blocks. When defeated, guardians can drop prismarine shards, prismarine crystals, and raw cod.

Hoglins are hostile mobs that spawn in the Crimson Forest biome in the Nether. They are large, boar-like creatures with tusks and are one of the few sources of food in the Nether. Hoglins attack players on sight and can knock them back with their powerful charge attack. They're afraid of warped fungi and will run away from them. Hoglins can be bred using crimson fungi, and baby hoglins are neutral instead of hostile. When defeated, they drop raw porkchop and leather. If a hoglin stays in the Overworld for too long (about 15 seconds), it will transform into a zoglin, which is even more aggressive.

Horses are passive, tamable mobs that spawn in plains, savannas, and meadows. They come in various colors and patterns, including white, black, brown, chestnut, creamy, and dark brown, with five different marking patterns. Horses can be tamed by repeatedly mounting them until hearts appear, and then can be ridden using a saddle. Each horse has unique statistics for health, speed, and jump height. They can be bred using golden apples or golden carrots, and can be equipped with armor for protection. Horses can be led with a lead and tied to a fence post, and they'll follow players holding their favorite foods.

Husks are hostile mobs that are desert variants of zombies. They spawn in desert biomes and, unlike regular zombies, don't burn in sunlight. Husks have a distinctive appearance with tattered desert clothing and sand-colored skin. When a husk attacks a player, it inflicts the hunger effect in addition to dealing damage. If a husk drowns in water, it transforms into a regular zombie. Like zombies, husks can spawn wearing armor and can break down doors on hard difficulty. Baby husks are faster and more dangerous than their adult counterparts. When defeated, husks drop rotten flesh and have a small chance to drop iron ingots, carrots, or potatoes.

Illusioners are unused hostile illager mobs that don't spawn naturally in the game and can only be summoned using commands. They are spellcasters, similar to evokers, but with different magical abilities. Illusioners can create multiple illusory copies of themselves to confuse players and attack with a bow and arrow from a distance. They also have the ability to turn invisible temporarily, making them difficult to track. Despite being fully implemented in the game's code, they were never officially added to the natural spawning pool. When defeated, illusioners can drop bows and emeralds.

Iron Golems are utility mobs that protect villages and villagers from hostile mobs. They can spawn naturally in villages with a certain number of villagers and beds, or they can be created by players by placing four iron blocks in a T-shape and a carved pumpkin or jack-o'-lantern on top. Iron Golems are tall, powerful mobs made of iron with vines growing on their bodies. They're passive toward players with high village popularity but will become hostile if a player attacks them or a villager. Iron Golems attack by swinging their long arms and can launch enemies into the air. They occasionally offer poppies to villager children.

Llamas are passive, tamable mobs that spawn in savanna and mountainous biomes. They come in four different coat patterns: creamy, white, brown, and gray. Llamas can't be ridden directly, but they can be equipped with chests to carry items and can be led in caravans using a lead. When a llama is led, other nearby llamas will follow, forming a caravan of up to 10 llamas. Each llama has a random strength value (1-5) that determines how many inventory slots its chest will have. Llamas defend themselves by spitting at hostile mobs or players who attack them. They can be bred using hay bales, and baby llamas take 20 minutes to grow into adults.

Magma Cubes are hostile mobs that are the Nether variant of slimes. They spawn in the Nether, particularly in nether wastes, basalt deltas, and nether fortresses. Magma Cubes have a distinctive appearance with a molten, fiery body made of magma blocks. Like slimes, they come in different sizes (small, medium, and large), and when defeated, they split into smaller versions of themselves. Unlike slimes, Magma Cubes are immune to fire and lava damage and can swim in lava. They attack by jumping at players and dealing contact damage. When defeated, they have a chance to drop magma cream, which is used in brewing fire resistance potions and crafting magma blocks.

Mooshrooms (also called Mushroom Cows) are passive mobs that exclusively spawn in the rare Mushroom Fields biome. They have a distinctive appearance with mushrooms growing on their backs. Mooshrooms come in two variants: red (the original) and brown (created when a red mooshroom is struck by lightning). They can be milked like regular cows, but they also have special interactions: using a bowl on a red mooshroom gives mushroom stew, while using it on a brown mooshroom gives suspicious stew with a random effect. Using shears on a mooshroom turns it into a regular cow and drops 5 mushrooms. Mooshrooms can be bred using wheat, and baby mooshrooms take 20 minutes to grow.

Mules are hybrid mobs created by breeding a horse with a donkey. They combine traits from both parents, having the speed of horses and the strength of donkeys. Like donkeys, mules can be equipped with chests to carry items, making them valuable for transportation and storage during exploration. Mules cannot be bred with other mules or any other mob. They can be tamed by repeatedly mounting them until hearts appear, and then can be ridden using a saddle. Mules are slightly faster than donkeys but slower than horses, and they can jump reasonably high. When defeated, they drop leather.

Ocelots are shy, passive mobs that spawn in jungle biomes. They have a distinctive spotted yellow-orange coat and are one of the fastest animals in the game. Unlike cats, ocelots cannot be tamed but can be bred using raw fish. They're naturally afraid of players and will run away when approached, but can be slowly coaxed closer using raw cod or salmon. Ocelots are immune to fall damage and will attack chickens and baby turtles. They're also one of the few mobs that phantoms will avoid, making them useful for keeping these night predators away. When defeated, ocelots have a chance to drop string.

Pandas are passive mobs that spawn in jungle and bamboo jungle biomes. They come in seven different personality types, each with unique behaviors: normal, lazy, worried, playful, aggressive, weak, and brown. Each personality affects how the panda behaves - lazy pandas lie on their backs, playful ones roll around, and aggressive ones attack players who harm other pandas. Pandas love bamboo and can be bred using it, though breeding is only successful if at least one parent is normal or has the same personality as the other. Baby pandas have a chance to inherit a recessive gene from their parents. Pandas sneeze occasionally, startling nearby pandas, and drop bamboo when defeated. They're one of the most expressive and animated mobs in the game.

Parrots are colorful, tamable mobs that spawn in jungle biomes. They come in five different color variants: red, blue, green, cyan, and gray. Parrots can be tamed using seeds, after which they will follow the player and can perch on their shoulder when the player walks into them. They have a unique ability to imitate the sounds of nearby hostile mobs, which can serve as a warning system for players. Parrots will dance when near a jukebox playing music, bobbing their heads and lifting their legs in rhythm. They're vulnerable to cookies, which are toxic to them and will cause instant death if fed to a parrot. When defeated, parrots drop feathers.

Phantoms are hostile flying mobs that spawn in the night sky when a player hasn't slept for three or more in-game days. These undead creatures have a unique appearance with translucent blue-green skin and large wings. They attack by swooping down at players from above, making them particularly dangerous when players are in exposed areas or at height. Phantoms are vulnerable to sunlight and will catch fire in daylight, similar to zombies and skeletons. They're afraid of cats and ocelots, which will cause them to keep their distance. When defeated, phantoms drop phantom membranes, which are used to repair elytra and brew Slow Falling potions. Their spawn mechanic encourages players to maintain a regular sleep schedule in-game.

Pigs are passive farm animals that spawn in grassy biomes in the Overworld. They can be bred using carrots, potatoes, or beetroot, and drop porkchops when defeated. Pigs can be ridden using a saddle, though they can't be controlled without a carrot on a stick. If struck by lightning, pigs transform into zombie piglins. Baby pigs follow adult pigs, and pigs can be led using vegetables. They're one of the oldest mobs in Minecraft, having been added in the Survival Test phase.

Piglins are neutral humanoid mobs native to the Nether. They're obsessed with gold and won't attack players wearing gold armor. Players can barter with piglins by giving them gold ingots, which causes them to drop various items in return. Piglins attack players who mine gold or open chests near them. They hunt hoglins for food and are afraid of soul fire and zombified piglins. If a piglin stays in the Overworld for too long (about 15 seconds), it transforms into a zombified piglin. Baby piglins never grow up and are not hostile, but they still attack wither skeletons.

Piglin Brutes are hostile variants of piglins that spawn exclusively in bastion remnants in the Nether. Unlike regular piglins, they are always hostile to players, even when wearing gold armor. Piglin Brutes are stronger and have more health than regular piglins, and they attack with golden axes. They don't barter with players and can't be distracted by gold items. Like regular piglins, they transform into zombified piglins if they stay in the Overworld for too long. Piglin Brutes serve as guardians of bastion remnants and will attack players who open chests or break blocks containing gold.

Pillagers are hostile illager mobs that attack with crossbows. They spawn naturally at pillager outposts, during village raids, and occasionally patrol in groups near villages. Pillagers are recognizable by their gray skin, distinctive eyebrows, and crossbows. When a pillager captain (identified by a banner on its back) is defeated, it applies the Bad Omen effect to the player, which triggers a raid when the player enters a village. During raids, pillagers attack alongside other illager types to eliminate villagers. They're sdefeated with their crossbows and can shoot from a considerable distance, making them dangerous ranged enemies.

Polar Bears are neutral mobs that spawn in cold, snowy biomes and frozen oceans. They are typically passive unless players approach their cubs, at which point they become hostile and protective. Adult polar bears will attack if provoked, while cubs are always passive. They can swim in water and occasionally stand on their hind legs when idle. Polar bears drop raw cod or raw salmon when defeated, suggesting they hunt fish as their primary food source. They're one of the few mobs in Minecraft that exhibit protective parental behavior, making them unique among the game's wildlife.

Pufferfish are small, passive aquatic mobs that spawn in warm and lukewarm ocean biomes. They have a unique defense mechanism: when a player approaches, they inflate to about twice their normal size and become spiky. In this inflated state, they can poison and damage players who touch them. Pufferfish can be caught in a water bucket or with a fishing rod. When defeated, they drop pufferfish items, which can be used to brew Potions of Water Breathing but are poisonous if eaten. They're one of the three fish mob types in Minecraft, alongside cod and salmon.

Rabbits are small passive mobs that spawn in various biomes and come in seven different coat colors based on their environment. They hop around quickly and can jump high, making them difficult to catch. Rabbits can be bred using carrots, golden carrots, or dandelions. When defeated, they drop rabbit hide, raw rabbit, and occasionally a rabbit's foot, which is used in brewing Potions of Leaping. There's also an extremely rare hostile variant called 'The Killer Bunny' that attacks players, but it can only be spawned using commands. In desert biomes, rabbits will attack and eat cacti, which is their only source of food in that environment.

Ravagers are large, powerful hostile mobs that appear during village raids. They resemble a cross between a bull and a rhinoceros with a cage-like structure on their backs. Ravagers have high health and deal significant damage with their charging attack, which can break certain blocks like crops, leaves, and flowers. They can also roar to stun nearby players. During raids, other illagers like pillagers and vindicators can ride on ravagers, making them even more dangerous. Ravagers always drop a saddle when defeated and are considered one of the most formidable non-boss mobs in the game due to their high health, damage, and knockback resistance.

Salmon are passive aquatic mobs that spawn in cold oceans, frozen oceans, and rivers. They have a distinctive reddish-orange color and are larger than cod. Salmon swim in schools and will flee from players and predators. They can be caught with a water bucket to create a bucket of salmon, which can be placed to release the salmon. When defeated, they drop raw salmon and occasionally bone meal. Salmon are an important food source in the game and can also be fed to cats and foxes. Unlike real salmon, they don't swim upstream to spawn, but they do prefer colder water biomes.

Sheep are passive farm animals that spawn in various grassy biomes in the Overworld. They come in 16 different colors, with white being the most common naturally. Sheep can be dyed any color using dyes, and they pass their color to offspring when bred. Their most distinctive feature is their ability to be sheared for wool, which regrows after eating grass. Sheep can be bred using wheat, and baby sheep take 20 minutes to grow into adults. When defeated, sheep drop mutton and wool of their color. They make a distinctive 'baa' sound and will follow players holding wheat. Sheep were one of the first passive mobs added to Minecraft.
(Depends on color of the sheep)

Shulkers are hostile box-like mobs that spawn in end cities. They have a unique defensive mechanism where they can close their shell to become completely invulnerable to damage. When open, they shoot homing projectiles that cause the Levitation effect, making players float upward and potentially take fall damage. Shulkers can teleport short distances when attacked, making them difficult to defeat. They come in various colors, with purple being the most common. When defeated, they have a chance to drop shulker shells, which are used to craft shulker boxes - valuable storage items that retain their contents when broken. Shulkers were added in the 1.9 Combat Update.

Silverfish are small hostile mobs that hide in special blocks called 'infested blocks' which look identical to regular stone, cobblestone, stone bricks, and other similar blocks. When these blocks are broken, the silverfish emerges and attacks. They're known for their ability to call nearby silverfish for help when injured, potentially causing a swarm to emerge from surrounding blocks. Silverfish are commonly found in strongholds, woodland mansions, and igloo basements. They move quickly, can fit through 1-block gaps, and don't drop any items when defeated. Their name comes from the real-life silverfish insect, which they somewhat resemble.

Skeletons are common hostile undead mobs that attack players with bows and arrows from a distance. They spawn in dark areas throughout the Overworld and burn in direct sunlight unless wearing a helmet or standing in water. Skeletons are sdefeated archers, with their accuracy increasing with difficulty level. They can ride spiders to create spider jockeys, a rare and dangerous combination. When defeated, skeletons drop bones, arrows, and occasionally bows. They're one of the oldest hostile mobs in Minecraft and have several variants, including strays, wither skeletons, and skeleton horsemen. Skeletons fear wolves and will actively flee from them.

Skeleton Horses are rare undead equine mobs that primarily spawn during thunderstorms as part of a 'skeleton trap'. When a player approaches what appears to be a lone skeleton horse, it triggers the trap, causing lightning to strike and transform it into four skeleton horsemen - skeleton horses mounted by skeletons equipped with enchanted bows and helmets. Unlike regular horses, skeleton horses can be ridden underwater, as they don't need to surface for air. They can be tamed immediately without the need for food, but cannot be bred or fed. Skeleton horses don't take damage from fire or drowning, but they do take damage from the Instant Health effect and healing potions, like other undead mobs.

Slimes are hostile, gelatinous cube-shaped mobs that come in three sizes: small, medium, and large. They spawn in swamp biomes at night (with light level and moon phase affecting spawn rates) and in specific underground chunks below Y=40. When defeated, large and medium slimes split into 2-4 smaller slimes, while small slimes drop slimeballs, which are used to craft items like sticky pistons and lead. Slimes move by jumping and deal damage by colliding with players. Only medium and large slimes are hostile; small slimes can't deal damage. Slimes make distinctive squishing sounds when moving and can be detected through walls by their sound. They're the Overworld counterpart to the Nether's magma cubes.

The Sniffer is a large, ancient passive mob that was added in the 1.20 update after winning the 2022 Mob Vote. These prehistoric creatures can only be obtained by finding and hatching Sniffer eggs, which are discovered through archaeology at ocean ruins. Sniffers have a unique ability to sniff out and dig up rare, ancient seeds that can't be obtained any other way. These seeds grow into decorative plants like torchflowers and pitcher plants. Sniffers have a distinctive appearance with mossy fur, six legs, and a long snout. They can be bred using torchflower seeds to produce eggs, making them renewable once obtained. Sniffers are the largest passive mob in the game.

Snow Golems are utility mobs that players can create by stacking two snow blocks and placing a carved pumpkin or jack o'lantern on top. They attack hostile mobs by throwing snowballs, which don't deal damage but can knock back enemies and attract the attention of other hostile mobs. Snow Golems leave a trail of snow behind them as they move and will slowly melt in biomes with a temperature above 1.0, such as deserts and the Nether. They take damage from water and rain. Players can use shears to remove their pumpkin head, revealing a derpy snow face underneath. When defeated, they drop snowballs and occasionally a snow block. They're one of the few mobs that players can create.

Spiders are common hostile mobs that spawn in dark areas throughout the Overworld. They have a unique ability to climb walls and can jump up to 4 blocks horizontally. Unlike most hostile mobs, spiders become neutral in daylight (light level 12 or higher), only attacking when provoked. They make distinctive hissing sounds when aggressive. Spiders can occasionally spawn with a skeleton rider, creating a dangerous 'spider jockey' combination. When defeated, they drop string and sometimes spider eyes, which are used in brewing and crafting. Spiders are 1 block tall but nearly 2 blocks wide, allowing them to fit through spaces that are 1 block high but requiring wider passages than most mobs.

Squids are passive aquatic mobs that spawn in oceans, rivers, and underground lakes. They have a distinctive dark blue color and move by propelling themselves through water with their tentacles. When attacked or beached, squids release a cloud of ink and attempt to flee. They can survive on land but will slowly take suffocation damage if they remain out of water for too long. When defeated, squids drop ink sacs, which are used for crafting black dye and other items. Squids were one of the first aquatic mobs added to Minecraft and have a glowing variant called the glow squid that was added in a later update. Unlike real squids, they don't have any predators in the game and don't actively hunt for food.

Strays are skeleton variants that spawn in cold, snowy biomes. They have a distinctive appearance with tattered blue-white clothing and emit cold vapor particles. Unlike regular skeletons, strays shoot arrows tipped with the Slowness effect, making them more dangerous as they can reduce player movement speed. They burn in sunlight like other undead mobs unless wearing a helmet or standing in water. Strays can also be created when a skeleton is submerged in powdered snow for 15 seconds. They drop the same loot as skeletons, with the addition of tipped arrows of slowness, making them a valuable target for players seeking these special arrows.
(Slowness)

Striders are passive mobs native to the Nether that can walk on lava without taking damage. They have a unique appearance with long legs, red flesh, and shiver when not in lava, turning purple when cold. Players can ride striders using a saddle and control them with a warped fungus on a stick, making them valuable for traversing the Nether's lava oceans. Baby striders follow adults, and striders can be bred using warped fungi. They're one of the few peaceful mobs in the Nether and were added in the Nether Update. When defeated, striders drop string and sometimes warped fungi.

Tadpoles are small, passive aquatic mobs that represent the juvenile stage of frogs in Minecraft. They spawn from frogspawn placed by frogs in water and swim in groups. Tadpoles grow into frogs after about 20 minutes, with their final color determined by the biome they mature in: temperate (orange), cold (green), or warm (white). They can be caught in buckets and transported, allowing players to raise frogs in different biomes to get specific variants. Tadpoles are prey to axolotls and can suffocate if they leave water. They were added in the Wild Update alongside frogs and represent one of the few mobs with a complete life cycle in the game.

Trader Llamas are special variants of llamas that spawn alongside Wandering Traders. They have distinctive blue carpet decorations and are leashed to the trader. Unlike regular llamas, Trader Llamas cannot be bred and will despawn along with their trader after a period of time. They defend themselves and their trader by spitting at hostile mobs or players who attack them. If a player kills the Wandering Trader, the Trader Llamas become unleashed and can then be tamed like regular llamas. They can be equipped with chests for storage and form caravans when led. Trader Llamas come in the same four color variants as regular llamas: creamy, white, brown, and gray.

Tropical Fish are colorful passive aquatic mobs that spawn in warm and lukewarm oceans, as well as lush caves. They come in 3,584 possible combinations of colors, patterns, and sizes, making them the most visually diverse mob in Minecraft. Each fish has a base color, pattern color, and one of 6 distinct body patterns (including angelfish, butterflyfish, and clownfish). They swim in schools and flee from players and predators. Tropical Fish can be caught with a water bucket to create a bucket of tropical fish, which preserves the fish's unique pattern and colors when placed. When defeated, they drop a tropical fish item (which always appears as a clownfish regardless of the actual fish's appearance) and occasionally bone meal.

Turtles are passive mobs that spawn on beaches in the Overworld. They have a unique breeding mechanic where they always return to their home beach to lay eggs, regardless of how far they've traveled. Turtles can be bred using seagrass, and baby turtles drop scutes when they grow into adults, which are used to craft turtle shells for water breathing helmets. Adult turtles are slow on land but swim quickly in water. They're immune to most types of damage while retracting into their shells. Turtles are one of the few mobs that players are discouraged from killing, as they drop only seagrass and bowls, making scute farming through breeding more valuable. They're also hunted by zombies, skeletons, and wolves, adding an element of protection to their gameplay.

Vexes are small, hostile flying mobs that are summoned by Evokers during combat. These ghostly entities have a pale blue, translucent appearance with small wings and wield iron swords. Vexes can phase through solid blocks, making them difficult to escape from or trap. They attack in quick swooping motions and have a unique charging attack where they briefly turn red before lunging at their target. Vexes have limited lifespans, despawning after about 30-120 seconds, but are dangerous due to their high damage and ability to attack from unexpected angles. They don't drop any items when defeated, making them purely a combat challenge. Vexes are one of the main reasons Evokers are considered among the most dangerous illager types.

Villagers are intelligent passive mobs that inhabit villages throughout the Minecraft world. They come in five different appearance types based on their biome: plains, desert, savanna, taiga, and snow. Villagers have a complex profession system with 13 different jobs (including farmer, librarian, cleric, and armorer), each with their own unique trades and workstation blocks. They level up through trading, offering better deals at higher levels. Villagers can breed when there are sufficient beds in a village, and baby villagers take 20 minutes to grow up. They seek shelter at night, hiding in houses to avoid zombies, which can convert them into zombie villagers. Villagers are protected by iron golems and can gossip about players, affecting reputation and trade prices. They're one of the most sophisticated mobs in the game, with their own economy, schedules, and social behaviors.

Vindicators are hostile illager mobs that attack with iron axes. They spawn naturally in woodland mansions and participate in village raids. Vindicators are aggressive melee fighters that charge directly at players with high speed and deal significant damage with their axes. They have a unique mechanic where naming one 'Johnny' with a name tag causes it to attack all other mobs except other illagers. During raids, vindicators can break down doors to reach villagers. They're distinguished by their gray skin, blue robes, and angry expressions. When defeated, vindicators may drop emeralds and occasionally their iron axes. They're considered one of the more dangerous illager types due to their high damage output and aggressive behavior.

Wandering Traders are nomadic merchants that randomly appear near players in the Overworld. They spawn with two leashed trader llamas and offer a variety of uncommon items, particularly biome-specific blocks and materials that might be difficult for players to obtain otherwise. Wandering Traders have a limited trading inventory that refreshes with each new trader, and they don't restock like villagers. They stay in an area for about 40-60 minutes before despawning, and will drink a Potion of Invisibility at night or when attacked. Unlike villagers, they can't be bred or assigned professions. Wandering Traders provide a unique way for players to acquire items from distant biomes without extensive travel, making them valuable encounters despite their temporary nature.

The Warden is one of the most dangerous mobs in Minecraft, introduced in the Wild Update. This blind, hostile creature spawns in the Deep Dark biome when players trigger too many sculk shriekers. Despite being blind, the Warden has exceptional hearing and can detect players through vibrations and smell. It becomes progressively angrier as it detects more disturbances, indicated by the sculk-like heart on its chest pulsing faster and changing color from blue to red. The Warden has the highest health of any mob in the game (500) and deals massive damage (30 hearts) with each hit. It can also unleash a sonic boom ranged attack that bypasses armor and walls. The Warden is designed as an obstacle to avoid rather than defeat, encouraging stealth gameplay. When defeated, it drops sculk and echo shards, which are used to craft recovery compasses.

Witches are hostile mobs that attack players by throwing harmful potions. They spawn naturally in swamps, witch huts, and during village raids. Witches have a distinctive appearance with a large nose, wart, purple robes, and a pointed hat. They're unique among hostile mobs for their defensive potion drinking - they'll consume potions of healing when injured, fire resistance when burning, and water breathing when drowning. When attacking, witches throw various negative effect potions including poison, slowness, weakness, and harming. They can also throw splash potions at themselves to gain positive effects. Witches drop a variety of brewing ingredients when defeated, making them valuable targets for players interested in potion-making. During raids, they can heal other illagers with splash potions of healing.

The Wither is a powerful undead boss mob that players can summon by arranging soul sand or soul soil in a T-shape and placing three wither skeleton skulls on top. This three-headed floating entity has a central head and two side heads, all capable of firing explosive wither skulls that cause the wither effect, which damages entities and prevents healing. The Wither has 300 health points and is immune to fire, lava, and drowning. When first summoned, it creates a massive explosion and is temporarily invulnerable while charging up. The Wither can break most blocks in its path and will actively hunt down any nearby living entities. When reduced to half health, it gains a protective shield that makes it immune to arrows. Upon defeat, it drops a nether star, which is essential for crafting beacons. The Wither is considered the second most powerful mob in Minecraft after the Ender Dragon.

Wither Skeletons are tall, black skeletal mobs that exclusively spawn in Nether Fortresses. They stand 2.4 blocks tall, making them significantly taller than regular skeletons. Unlike their Overworld counterparts, Wither Skeletons attack with stone swords in melee combat rather than bows. Their attacks inflict the Wither effect, which causes damage over time and prevents natural health regeneration. Wither Skeletons are immune to fire and lava damage, making them well-adapted to the Nether environment. They drop bones, coal, and occasionally their swords when defeated. Most importantly, they have a rare chance (2.5%) to drop Wither Skeleton Skulls, which are essential components for summoning the Wither boss. Wither Skeletons will attack Piglins on sight but are ignored by other Nether mobs.

Wolves are neutral mobs that spawn in forest and taiga biomes. They can be tamed using bones, transforming them into loyal companions with red collars that can be dyed different colors. Untamed wolves will attack players who hit them, while tamed wolves will follow their owners and attack hostile mobs or players who harm their owner. Tamed wolves can be healed with any type of meat and will sit or follow based on player commands. They take less damage than other mobs and have a distinctive appearance change when wet. Wolves are natural enemies of skeletons and rabbits, which they will chase and attack on sight. They can be bred using any meat to produce puppies, which take 20 minutes to grow into adults. Wolves were one of the first tamable mobs added to Minecraft and remain one of the most popular pets in the game.
(If slain by a player)

Zoglins are the undead versions of hoglins that appear when hoglins remain outside the Nether for more than 15 seconds. These aggressive mobs have a decayed, zombie-like appearance with exposed bones and rotting flesh. Unlike hoglins, zoglins attack all mobs except other undead creatures, making them dangerous to both players and most other entities. They cannot be bred or tamed, and baby zoglins (transformed from baby hoglins) never grow up. Zoglins are immune to fire and lava damage like other undead mobs, but they take damage from the Instant Health effect and are healed by Instant Damage. When defeated, they drop rotten flesh instead of the porkchops that hoglins provide. Zoglins represent one of the few mobs that are created through a transformation process rather than natural spawning.

Zombies are undead hostile mobs that hunt for players and villagers. They spawn in groups in the Overworld at night, or in dark areas like caves. Zombies will burn in sunlight unless they wear a helmet or are in water. They can break down doors on Hard difficulty and will sometimes spawn wearing armor. Baby zombies are faster and more dangerous than their adult counterparts.

Zombie Horses are undead equine mobs that cannot spawn naturally in the game and can only be summoned using commands. They have a decayed, greenish appearance with exposed bones and rotting flesh. Unlike regular horses, Zombie Horses cannot be tamed, bred, or equipped with armor. They can, however, be ridden if a saddle is placed on them using commands. As undead mobs, they're immune to drowning, fire, and lava damage, but take damage from the Instant Health effect and are healed by Instant Damage. When defeated, they drop rotten flesh. Zombie Horses were originally intended to be part of the 'skeleton trap' mechanic that now only uses skeleton horses. Despite being fully implemented in the game's code, they remain one of the few mobs that are inaccessible in survival gameplay without commands.

Zombie Villagers are undead variants of villagers that retain their profession-specific clothing and features. They spawn naturally in the Overworld or when a zombie kills a villager (with a 100% chance on Hard difficulty). Unlike regular zombies, Zombie Villagers can be cured and transformed back into normal villagers through a specific process: first weakening them with a Splash Potion of Weakness, then feeding them a Golden Apple. The curing process takes about 2-5 minutes, during which they tremble and emit red particles. Cured villagers remember their previous profession and offer better trading deals to the player who cured them. Zombie Villagers have the same behavior as regular zombies - they burn in sunlight unless wearing a helmet, can break down doors on Hard difficulty, and attack players and villagers. They drop the same loot as zombies when defeated.

Zombified Piglins are neutral mobs that spawn in the Nether and are the zombified version of Piglins. They are created when a Piglin is defeated by a zombie or when a player kills a Piglin while wearing no gold armor. Zombified Piglins are unique in that they will not attack players unless provoked, and they can be found wandering around the Nether, often in groups. They have a distinctive appearance with greenish skin and golden armor pieces. When defeated, they drop gold nuggets and can also drop gold ingots if they were wearing any.
screen.araxers_bestiary.rankx.gates_need: Reach Rank S to begin gates
